caption_tesim: Text from the negative sleeve St. Francis Text from negative sleeve: St. Francis Dam. 3-18-28.

description_tesim: The St. Francis Dam was a 200-foot high concrete gravity-arch dam built between 1924 and 1926 in St. Francisquito Canyon (near present-day Castaic and Santa Clarita). The dam collapsed on March 12, 1928 at two and a half minutes before midnight. The resulting flood killed more than 600 residents plus an unknown number of itinerant farm workers camped in San Francisquito Canyon, making it the 2nd greatest loss of life in California after the 1906 San Francisco earthquake. It is considered the worst American civil engineering failure in the 20th century.
View of the largest remaining portion of the Saint Francis Dam after its collapse, known as the tombstone section

The St. Francis Dam was a 200-foot high concrete gravity-arch dam built between 1924 and 1926 in St. Francisquito Canyon (near present-day Castaic and Santa Clarita). The dam collapsed on March 12, 1928 at two and a half minutes before midnight. The resulting flood killed more than 600 residents plus an unknown number of itinerant farm workers camped in San Francisquito Canyon, making it the 2nd greatest loss of life in California after the 1906 San Francisco earthquake. It is considered the worst American civil engineering failure in the 20th century.

description_tesim: Downward view towards the stepped face of the largest remaining portion of the Saint Francis Dam after its collapse and the canyon beyond.

The St. Francis Dam was a 200-foot high concrete gravity-arch dam built between 1924 and 1926 in St. Francisquito Canyon (near present-day Castaic and Santa Clarita). The dam collapsed on March 12, 1928 at two and a half minutes before midnight. The resulting flood killed more than 600 residents plus an unknown number of itinerant farm workers camped in San Francisquito Canyon, making it the 2nd greatest loss of life in California after the 1906 San Francisco earthquake. It is considered the worst American civil engineering failure in the 20th century.

title_tesim: Letter on Hotel Brandon stationery accompanying photographs of weirs in the Fort Sumner area, 1940
caption_tesim: [In pencil:] 3/18/1940. [In ink:] These are the pictures Arthur Morgan took of the control weirs in Ft. Sumner area. We'll your have printed what you want and 2 each for me as I would like to use in my progress report. Of course Warga [sp] will want the films back some time but no hurry as he has to account for them. [In pencil:] 10 prints. March 25 1940. Films attached H7B took 2 prints each of 5 to Carlsbad. [Stationary heading:] Dudley Yard, Owner - Manager, Member American Hotel Association, Texas Hotel Association, Official AAA Hotel, Hotel Brandon, Air Conditioned, "The Home on the Range," Pecos, Texas, Gateway of the Carlsbad Caverns and Big Bend Country.

title_tesim: United Nations Under-Secretary Bunche comes to Gaza
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: http://calcultures.cdlib.com
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
rights_holder_tesim: UN Photo
United Nations Publications 2 UN Plaza, DC2 Room 856 New York, NY 10017, USA
description_tesim: "UN 53656 -- Gaza, 11 March 1957 -- U.N. Under-Secretary Ralph Bunche today came to Gaza for a 24-hour visit. He is shown here stepping off his plane, after arriving by air from Abu Suweir, the base camp of the U.N. Emergency Force (UNEF) in Egypt. He was accompanied by Maj. Gen. E.L.M. Burns, Commander of UNEF. Dr. Bunche will visit some of the units in the Gaza area he will also confer with staff officers of the Force and officials of the U.N. Relief & Works Agency for Palestine Refugees (UNRWA)."
Digitization took place as part of the California Cultures Project.
member_of_collections_ssim: Urquhart (Brian) Collection of Material about Ralph Bunche, ca. 1932-1972

description_tesim: Fragment containing parts or all of four short chapters 84 to 87, including: verses 16 to 25 of al-inshiqāq (The sundering), all of al-Burῡj (The mansions of the stars), all of al-Ṭāriq (The morning star), and verses 1 through 14 and the first two words of verse 15 from al-A̒alá (The most high).

title_tesim: Kanz al-lughāt = Treasure of words
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Exhibit Collection of Near Eastern manuscripts 1492-1848 (Collection 1656). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
summary_tesim: Persian dictionary of Koranic Arabic and Hadith terms. The author praises Sulṭan Muḥammad and his heir (2a) and calls them "The adornment of the Hall of the Musṭafawῑ lineage, and the decoration of the pride of the Murtaḍawῑ family," implying a Sayyid king, possibly Ṣafawid. If so, the named Sulṭan would be Sulṭan Muḥammad Khudābandah father of Shāh  ̒Abbās, and the date of composition would fall between 1577-1586 CE.
description_tesim: Minasian Collection of Near Eastern Manuscripts (Collection 1147), 1147/1Ms. 1057/1.
Author: Muḥammad ibn ̒Abd al-Khāliq ibn Ma ̒arῡf, fl. 16th cent?Scribe:Ibn Abῡ Turāb Muḥammad Ibrāhῑm (al-Ḥusayn?).Origin: Ṣafawid Persia

summary_tesim: Correspondence manual. This is a handbook of official correspondence, meant for secretaries, providing samples of letters to various government functionaries or potentates according to rank or the occasion. These include not only letters addressed to Sultans (3b), letters to Sultans on occasion of victories (4b), letters addressed to supreme Emirs (9b), viziers (11b), judges (16b), etc.; but also how to answer such letters.
description_tesim: Minasian Collection of Near Eastern Manuscripts (Collection 1147), 1147/5.

title_tesim: Solinus (C. Iulius) De situ orbis terrarum [et] de singulis mirabilibus quae in mundo habentur. Uibius Sequester De fluminibus montibus lacubus [et] gentibus : prouinciarum totius orbis nomina ; ad nostra tempora redacta.
alternative_title_tesim: De fluminibus fontibus lacubus nemoribus paludibus montibus et gentibus
De situ orbis terrarum et de singulis mirabilibus quae in mundo habentur
De fluminibus montibus lacubus et gentibus
Vibii Sequestris De fluminibus fontibus lacubus nemoribus paludibus montibus et gentibus
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
summary_tesim: An annotated copy of De situ orbis terrarum ... [Polyhistor] by the 3rd or 4th century grammarian and compiler, C. Julius Solinus. This 1512 edition has been extensively annotated by Camillo Capilupi.
description_tesim: "Tabula": leaf [2]-[5] (1st group).

Spec. Coll. copy: Copious marginal annotations by Camillo Capilupi [?], with his signature "Camilli Gradi" at the end, and note indicating price he paid for the book: "Emptu[m] 13 18". In old wooden boards, brown calf spine; modern leather clasps and ties; rebound by René Patron. Purchase, E.P. Goldschmidt, 1986.
Manzoni, G. Annali tipografici dei Soncino, no. 70 Adams S-1390 BM STC Italian, 1465-1600, p. 632

At head of title: Haec continentur in hoc codice. Caption title, leaf [1] (3rd group): Vibii Sequestris De fluminibus fontibus lacubus nemoribus paludibus montibus et gentibus. Edited by Alexander Gaboardus. Imprint from leaf XXXIIII. Signatures: A⁶ a⁸ b-d⁶ e⁸, A-B⁴. Leaves printed on both sides. Capital spaces with guide letters. Verso of final leaf blank. "Tabula": leaf [2]-[5] (1st group) Errata: leaf [47]v-[48].
Errata: leaf [47] (verso)-[48].
